title: Cephaelis flaviflora
taxonomy: Cephaelis flaviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes around 600 genera and 13,000 species. The family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Cephaelis flaviflora is also known as the yellow-flowered cephaelis, a name that reflects its distinctive bright yellow flowers.

intro: Cephaelis flaviflora, commonly known as Yellow Gentian, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with malaria. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis flaviflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as quinine, a key compound responsible for its antimalarial activity. The most common herbal preparations made from Cephaelis flaviflora include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases.
